description:
Align a time-series sequence of 10 drifted FITS images using AstroImageJ.

Starting State: 10 FITS images (frame_00.fits to frame_09.fits) are located in `~/AstroImages/raw/drift_series/`. Due to simulated telescope tracking drift, the stars move significantly across the field of view.

Expected Actions:
1. Launch AstroImageJ.
2. Navigate to File > Import > Image Sequence... and select the `drift_series` directory to load all 10 images.
3. Open Process > Align stack using WCS or apertures.
4. Accept the default aperture settings and click OK.
5. Click on any prominent star to select it as the alignment reference, then press Enter (or right-click) to execute the alignment across the stack.
6. Navigate to File > Save As > Image Sequence...
7. Set the format to FITS.
8. Save the aligned stack to `~/AstroImages/processed/aligned_series/` with the filename prefix `aligned_`.

The final output must be 10 correctly aligned FITS files in the processed directory.
